_meta.description: Player handbook topics for Canon Engine RPG
_meta.format: Markdown-friendly text blocks for in-game help system

sections.content: Welcome, adventurer! Canon Engine is a text-based RPG where your choices shape the world. This guide will help you begin your journey.

You'll choose a name, backstory, and starting stats. Stats range from 1-20:
- **STR** (Strength): Physical power, melee damage
- **DEX** (Dexterity): Agility, stealth, ranged attacks
- **INT** (Intelligence): Magic power, knowledge, crafting
- **CHA** (Charisma): Persuasion, intimidation, leadership
- **CON** (Constitution): Health, endurance, poison resistance
- **LCK** (Luck): Critical hits, loot quality, random events

Average stat is 10. Heroes typically have 12-16 in their primary stats.
Begin by exploring your starting location. Talk to NPCs, examine objects, and accept quests. Use 'look around' to see what's available, and 'help commands' for a full command list.

Combat is turn-based. Each round:
1. Initiative is rolled (DEX-based)
2. Each character takes their turn in order
3. On your turn: move + action + bonus action
4. Round ends when all characters have acted

To attack, use 'attack [target]'. You roll d20 + attack bonus vs target's AC:
- **Hit**: Roll damage dice + modifiers
- **Critical Hit**: Natural 20, double damage dice
- **Miss**: Attack fails to connect

Your AC (Armor Class) determines how hard you are to hit. Higher is better. Sources of AC:
- Base: 10 + DEX modifier
- Armor: Adds flat AC bonus
- Shield: +2 AC
- Spells and abilities: Various bonuses

Common status effects:
- **Poisoned**: Disadvantage on attacks, damage over time
- **Frightened**: Disadvantage, can't move closer to source
- **Stunned**: Can't act for duration
- **Prone**: Disadvantage on attacks, melee advantage against you
- **Burning**: Fire damage each turn
- **Frozen**: Speed halved, DEX disadvantage
Use 'inventory' to see what you carry. Items have weight - exceeding your carry capacity slows you down. STR determines carry weight: STR × 15 = max lbs.

Items come in tiers of rarity:
- **Dirt** (50%): Basic, common goods
- **Common** (25%): Standard equipment
- **Uncommon** (12%): Notable quality
- **Rare** (7%): Powerful or unique
- **Epic** (4%): Exceptional artifacts
- **Legendary** (1.8%): Items of legend
- **Mythical** (0.2%): Reality-shaping power
Use 'use [item]' to consume or activate items. Equipment is auto-equipped when you 'equip [item]'. Some items require specific conditions (combat, rest, etc.).
NPCs can join your party through quests, reputation, or story events. Each companion has their own stats, abilities, and personality.

Commands: 'companion info [name]', 'companion gear [name]', 'companion tactics [name]'

Companions can be set to: Aggressive, Defensive, Support, or Free Will AI.
Companions have morale affected by your decisions. High morale grants combat bonuses. Low morale may cause them to leave. Actions that align with their values increase morale.

- **Fetch**: Retrieve an item from a location
- **Kill**: Defeat specific enemies
- **Deliver**: Transport items between locations
- **Explore**: Discover new areas or secrets
- **Escort**: Protect an NPC during travel
- **Main Story**: Advance the saga narrative

Use 'quests' to see active quests. 'quest [name]' for details. Quests can have:
- Optional objectives (bonus rewards)
- Time limits (fail if expired)
- Moral choices (affects reputation and story)
Rewards include: gold, XP, reputation, items, and story progression. Some quests unlock new areas, merchants, or abilities.
Use 'craft [recipe]' when you have the required materials and tools. Crafting skills: Alchemy, Smithing, Tailoring, Enchanting, Survival.

Each recipe has a DC. Roll d20 + skill modifier:
- **Success**: Craft the item
- **Critical Success**: Enhanced item
- **Failure**: Materials wasted (sometimes partial result)
Materials come from: defeating enemies (monster parts), gathering in wilderness (herbs, ore), purchasing from merchants, or finding in containers.

Use 'hide' to attempt stealth. Roll d20 + DEX (Stealth) vs enemy Perception. While hidden:
- Advantage on attacks (sneak attack bonus)
- Can bypass enemies
- Can pickpocket or eavesdrop

Bonus: darkness (+2), distractions (+2), cover (+2), rain (+1)
Penalty: bright light (-2), heavy armor (-3), running (-2), open terrain (-2)
Committing crimes (theft, assault, murder) affects reputation. Witnesses report crimes. Bounties are placed. Guards will confront you. The Thieves' Circle can help with legal troubles... for a price.
Gold is the standard currency. 1 gold = 10 silver = 100 copper. Higher denominations exist in wealthy areas.
Merchants buy at ~50% base value and sell at ~110%. Haggle skill and reputation modify prices. Different merchants specialize in different goods.
Buy low in one region, sell high in another. Prices vary by location. Rare goods command premiums in areas that can't produce them. Watch for bandits on trade routes!
At 0 HP, you're incapacitated. At negative CON score, you're dead. Party members can stabilize you with DC 10 Medicine check.

Death isn't always permanent. Options include:
- Temple resurrection (expensive, reputation-gated)
- Rare resurrection items
- Quest-based revival
- Dark bargains with powerful entities

Each death has lasting effects:
- First death: Minor stat penalty (temporary)
- Subsequent deaths: Increasing penalties
- Permadeath mode: Game over (optional hardcore mode)
- Some deaths change the story permanently

- 'look' / 'l': Examine surroundings
- 'go [direction]' / 'n/s/e/w': Move
- 'enter [location]': Enter a building/dungeon
- 'examine [object]': Inspect something
- 'search': Search for hidden things

- 'attack [target]': Melee/ranged attack
- 'cast [spell] [target]': Use magic
- 'defend': Take defensive stance (+2 AC)
- 'flee': Attempt to escape combat
- 'use [item]': Use an item in combat

- 'talk [npc]': Start conversation
- 'trade [merchant]': Open shop
- 'accept [quest]': Accept a quest
- 'give [item] [npc]': Give item to NPC
- 'craft [recipe]': Craft an item

- 'stats': View character stats
- 'inventory' / 'inv': View inventory
- 'equip [item]': Equip gear
- 'skills': View skill tree
- 'map': View current map
- 'save': Save game
- 'help [topic]': Get help on a topic
